meaning: n.同等,(股票等)票面价值adj.票面的,平价的,平均的,标准的,
examples: He was five under par after the first round.
Duffy's primitive guitar playing is well below par.
The 27-year-old is perfectly poised, three shots behind the leader, despite a disappointing one-over-par 72 on day two.
Perry, 47 hit a four-under 67 to draw level with Axley (67) and Adamonis (66) on 15-under par.
